$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the ADOPT branch of v4's
ensureCharacterVault (Phase-2, the store-backed capstone).
Proves the startup-heal path: a character with NO linked vault but a populated
same-name 'character' store already present (e.g. a prior run created and
populated the vault but its link write was lost mid cloud-materialization). v4
searches doc_mount_points for storeType==='character' named '<name> Character
Vault', keeps those where vaultHasRequiredFiles (all six REQUIRED_VAULT_FILES
present), and — when EXACTLY ONE qualifies — ADOPTS it (linkCharacterToVault),
returning { created:false, adopted:true } instead of minting a duplicate. This is
critical post-cutover: creating fresh here would populate empty files and orphan
the good vault.
REACHED VIA THE PUBLIC API: repos.characters.update(id, {managedField}) on the
vault-less character -> applyDocumentStoreWriteOverlay -> provision-on-the-fly ->
ensureCharacterVault -> the ADOPT branch (the populated same-name store survives).
FIXTURE: built by running v4's REAL repos.characters.create(character) once, THEN
nulling ONLY the character's characterDocumentMountPointId (the vault + its mount
point stay intact, enabled, populated). Both sides operate on a COPY of this SAME
baked fixture; the character id is read back.
OP: { title, firstMessage } — a managed-field patch. Triggers the adopt, then a
properties.json read-modify-write onto the ADOPTED vault (the other three
property keys preserved). No slim column, so updatedAt NOT bumped by the
routing; the adopt's linkCharacterToVault DOES write the FK back (mint
updatedAt), so the slim row's updatedAt IS touched by the link.
REINDEX: the oracle drives the REAL update (no QUILLTAP_JOB_CHILD), so
reindexSingleFile runs; the differential pins the link chunkCount and excludes
doc_mount_chunks.
NORMALIZATION: minted-values remap with ONE shared id-map across all six tables.
The ADOPTED mount point is the SAME one baked at create, so exactly ONE mount
point exists on both sides (no duplicate) — the key adopt assertion. Timestamps
-> <ts>; chunkCount -> <cc>.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key. Safe to commit.

character.name: Evangeline
character.identity: A wandering scholar of forgotten tongues.
character.title: the scholar
